alerts.title: Predicted OTIF Fine — Walmart MABD Miss
alerts.description: Shipment #89921 routed via I-75 corridor. Severe weather system over TN predicted to delay arrival by 2 days, missing Walmart MABD. Transportation Agent calculates 91% fine probability at 3% COGS.

alerts.title: MRSL Violation Risk — Amazon Inbound Lot
alerts.description: Inbound lot #A930 for Whiskas Purrfectly Chicken has 34 days remaining shelf life. Amazon Vendor Central rulebook requires 45-day minimum MRSL. Retail Intelligence Agent flags rejection risk at the FC.

alerts.title: OMP Line Adherence Drop — Plant US01
alerts.description: Line 4 efficiency dropped to 66% following a packaging material shortage from Supplier S-042. Supply Planning Agent flags 4,200 case shortfall vs. this week's confirmed production schedule. SKU Z-REP 55920 most exposed.

alerts.title: POS Depletion Spike — Target National
alerts.description: Retail Intelligence Agent detects Target POS depletion running 42% above Anaplan consensus for Greenies Dental Treats. No promotional event logged in TPM. Demand Planning Agent flags possible unlogged regional endcap program.

alerts.title: FEFO Conflict — Kroger Allocation at Risk
alerts.description: DC-02 (Dallas) has 2,800 CS of Pedigree Small Dog on hand expiring in 18 days. FEFO logic requires these cases to ship first, but Kroger MRSL requires 30 days remaining. Cases will be stranded unless diverted to a lower-MRSL customer before expiry.

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: Demand Planning Agent flags POS depletion running 42% above Anaplan consensus. A regional endcap program appears to be driving incremental volume not captured in the TPM system. Full fulfillment risks stockout for Walmart (Tier 1) on the same SKU by Tuesday.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Accept 5,000 CS (83%). Backorder remaining 1,000 CS. Escalate to Demand Planning team to log unlogged promotional lift in Anaplan.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: $8,400 OTIF risk if over-allocated to Target at expense of Walmart

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: Retail Intelligence Agent confirms Walmart MRSL requirement is 50 days for this category. Current available inventory lot #W-2201 has 38 days remaining. Allocating current lot risks DC rejection and a chargeback. Next compliant production batch available in 3 days.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Hold current lot. Wait 3 days for compliant batch from Plant US02. Ship in full at 14,000 CS against MABD — window is not breached if shipped Thursday.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: $22,500 fine avoided if held. $34,000 chargeback risk if shipped non-compliant.

purchaseOrders.issue: Logistics Constraint — LTL Cost vs. MABD
purchaseOrders.issueDetail: Transportation Agent flags that fulfilling today requires LTL expedite at $5,200 above standard cost. Amazon MABD is not until next Wednesday. Consolidating with a full truckload departing tomorrow saves freight while still comfortably meeting the window.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Hold today. Consolidate 3,200 CS with full TL departing DC-04 tomorrow. Meets MABD Wednesday with $5,200 freight savings.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: $5,200 freight savings. Zero OTIF risk — MABD met comfortably.

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: Supply Planning Agent flags that OMP production variance at Plant US01 (packaging shortage, Supplier S-042) has reduced network-available inventory to 5,700 CS. This is 60% of requested quantity. Remaining 3,800 CS cannot be committed without impacting Tier 1 accounts.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Allocate 5,700 CS (60%). Short 3,800 CS. Notify Chewy account team immediately. Escalate Supplier S-042 packaging delay to Procurement.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: $14,000 revenue at risk on shorted cases. Procurement escalation required.

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: FEFO logic requires allocating the 2,800 CS lot expiring in 18 days first. However, Kroger MRSL is 30 days — this lot does not comply. Compliant inventory is available at DC-01 but requires an inter-DC transfer adding 1 day to lead time. MABD is in 4 days — transfer is feasible.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Do not allocate near-expiry lot to Kroger. Initiate inter-DC transfer from DC-01. Divert 2,800 CS near-expiry lot to Petsmart (MRSL 15 days — compliant) to avoid write-off.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: $9,500 inventory write-off avoided via diversion. MABD met on 4-day transfer.

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: Order is 12% above forecast. Orchestrator Agent confirms available inventory can cover this overage without impacting Tier 1 accounts. Near-expiry lot diversion from Kroger resolution (PO-005) provides compliant inventory for this allocation.
purchaseOrders.recommendedAction: Accept in full at 2,800 CS using near-expiry lot diverted from Kroger (MRSL compliant for Petsmart at 15-day requirement). Resolves two issues simultaneously.
purchaseOrders.financialImpact: Dual-resolution: Petsmart order filled, near-expiry write-off avoided.

purchaseOrders.issueDetail: No constraints detected across supply, shelf-life, logistics, or forecast. Orchestrator Agent auto-approved under standard guardrails.

fulfillmentIncidents.title: Shipment #89921 — Weather Delay
fulfillmentIncidents.description: Current routing via I-75 corridor predicts arrival 2 days past Walmart MABD due to severe weather system over TN. Retail Intelligence Agent has mapped Walmart OTIF rulebook: 3% COGS penalty on late shipments above $50K.
fulfillmentIncidents.otifRulebook: Walmart: 3% COGS on shipments arriving outside MABD window. No grace period for weather-related delays.

scenarios.name: Scenario B: Expedite LTL from DC-01
scenarios.rationale: Transportation Agent & Retail Intelligence Agent Joint Recommendation: Rerouting via DC-01 using LTL expedite avoids the I-75 weather corridor entirely. Higher freight cost is more than offset by the $22,500 OTIF fine avoided. DC-01 has confirmed inventory available for immediate pick.

executionSteps: Cancel outbound pick at DC-03 (Jacksonville) — Shipment #89921
Initiate emergency pick at DC-01 (Chicago) for 1,200 CS Pedigree Choice Cuts Beef 36ct
Book LTL carrier via carrier portal — Walmart DC Florida, deliver by Thursday 06:00
Update ASN in Walmart Retail Link portal — revised ship date and new pro number
Notify Walmart account team of routing change via EDI 214

fulfillmentIncidents.title: Carrier No-Show — Target DC (TX)
fulfillmentIncidents.description: Primary carrier failed to present for scheduled pickup at DC-02 (Dallas). 100% probability of out-of-stock event at Target distribution center tomorrow morning. Retail Intelligence Agent confirms Target is operating with minimal buffer stock on this SKU.
fulfillmentIncidents.otifRulebook: Target: $500 flat fee + 2% COGS for missed delivery appointments. Carrier no-show attributed to vendor.

scenarios.name: Scenario B: Hot-Shot Direct from Plant US01
scenarios.rationale: Transportation Agent Recommendation: Plant US01 has a finished goods buffer of 3,800 CS Greenies available for direct shipment. Hot-Shot carrier can reach Target DC (TX) within the MABD window. Higher freight is offset by the $14,000 fine avoided and the OOS event at Target prevented.

executionSteps: Issue hot-shot carrier PO via logistics portal — Plant US01 to Target DC Texas
Coordinate plant warehouse team for same-day pick and stage of 3,200 CS Greenies Dental Treats
Generate BOL and ASN — reference original Target PO #44019
Update Target IRIS portal with revised shipment details and pro number
File carrier no-show dispute with original carrier — attach documented timestamps

fulfillmentIncidents.title: Port Congestion — Amazon FC (PA) Inbound
fulfillmentIncidents.description: Port of Philadelphia congestion causing 5-day inbound delay on imported ingredients needed for production run. Risk of cutting prime fulfillment window for high-velocity promotional SKU ahead of Prime promotion event.
fulfillmentIncidents.otifRulebook: Amazon: POA required within 24hr of confirmed OTIF breach. Repeat violations subject to VC account suspension review.

scenarios.name: Scenario B: Substitute Domestic Ingredient + Expedite
scenarios.rationale: Supply Planning Agent & Transportation Agent Recommendation: Plant US01 has a qualified domestic ingredient substitute approved for this recipe. Switching to domestic sourcing for this run eliminates the port dependency. Expedited production and LTL direct to Amazon FC PA meets the Prime promotion window. Procurement team to confirm domestic supplier availability before execution.

executionSteps: Procurement to confirm domestic ingredient substitute availability with Supplier D-017
Issue revised production order at Plant US01 — substitute ingredient variant
Schedule production run for Wednesday — target finish goods availability Thursday AM
Book LTL carrier DC-04 to Amazon FC PA — target delivery Friday before promotion window
Submit Amazon Vendor Central ASN update with revised ship date

drivers.name: Unlogged Promotional Lift
drivers.description: Regional endcap program not logged in Anaplan TPM system caused 42% demand spike above consensus forecast. Resulted in 2,100 case shortfall across Target and Kroger allocations.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi Sarah,

We took a 2,100 case cut on Greenies Dental Treats last week tied to an unlogged promotional endcap at Target (regional). The spike was 42% above Anaplan consensus and wasn't visible until POS data came through on Tuesday — too late to reposition inventory.

Could you confirm whether this promotion was in the TPM system and, if not, ensure it gets logged retroactively? We'd also like to discuss how we flag in-market promos earlier so the Customer Supply team has advance notice.

This contributed to a $14,000 OTIF risk at Target this week.

Thanks

drivers.name: Anaplan Forecast Bias — Northeast Region
drivers.description: Systematic over-forecast in Northeast region for the last 3 weeks has caused inventory buildup in DC-04 while DC-03 ran short. Demand Planning Agent flagged statistical model bias — actual POS running 18% below consensus in Northeast, 22% above in Southeast.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi Sarah,

We're seeing a persistent regional forecast bias on Pedigree Choice Cuts Beef that's causing inventory to pool in the wrong DCs. Northeast is 18% below consensus for 3 consecutive weeks while Southeast is 22% above. The net result was 1,400 cut cases last week — inventory existed in the network but wasn't positioned correctly.

Would you be able to review the statistical model parameters for the Northeast region? We'd also like to explore whether we can get a weekly regional actuals vs. consensus report to catch this earlier.

Thanks

drivers.name: Phantom Demand — EDI Double-Order
drivers.description: Chewy automated reorder system triggered a duplicate EDI 850 during a minor system delay, effectively doubling the order. Customer Supply Agent caught the anomaly via order pattern comparison before fulfillment. 600 cases reallocated to other accounts.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi Marcus,

Heads up — Chewy's automated reorder system sent a duplicate EDI 850 on Whiskas Purrfectly Chicken last Tuesday during what looks like a brief delay in their system. We caught it before fulfilling the second order, which would have pulled 600 cases from other Tier 1 accounts.

It would be worth looping with the Chewy buyer to review their EDI reorder thresholds and add a duplicate-detection buffer. Happy to join that call if useful.

Thanks

drivers.name: Retailer Buffer Building — End of Quarter
drivers.description: Kroger placed a 28% above-forecast order in the final week of March, consistent with end-of-quarter DC buffer building behavior. Retail Intelligence Agent flagged pattern match to Q4 2025 behavior. 500 cases diverted from Dollar General allocation.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi Marcus,

Kroger placed a 28% above-forecast order on Pedigree Small Dog last week — the timing and pattern match their Q4 2025 end-of-quarter buffer build. We covered most of it but had to short Dollar General by 500 cases as a result.

This is the second quarter this behavior has impacted our CFR. Could we explore a VMI or order smoothing conversation with the Kroger buyer? I can pull the historical order pattern data to support the discussion.

Thanks

drivers.name: Raw Material Shortage — Supplier S-042
drivers.description: Supplier S-042 (flexible packaging) failed to deliver the scheduled inbound on time, causing a 2-day production halt on Line 4 at Plant US01. Supply Planning Agent flagged the risk 4 days in advance but escalation to procurement was delayed. Net impact: 2,200 case production cut.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi David,

The Line 4 halt at Plant US01 this week resulted in 2,200 cut cases across Greenies Dental Treats — our highest-velocity SKU. The root cause was a late delivery from Supplier S-042 on flexible packaging.

This is the second occurrence in 6 weeks. I'd like to discuss whether we should be reviewing S-042's SLA terms and whether we should qualify a secondary packaging supplier as a backup. The Supply Planning Agent flagged the risk 4 days ahead — we need a faster procurement escalation path to convert that signal into action.

Can we connect this week?

Thanks

drivers.name: Line Adherence Drop — Plant US01 Line 4
drivers.description: Line 4 at Plant US01 dropped to 66% efficiency following the packaging shortage event. Changeover time increased due to improvised material handling. Supply Planning Agent logged 900 additional cases lost to efficiency loss beyond the direct shortage impact.
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi Elena,

Beyond the direct material shortage impact, we logged an additional 900 case loss attributed to Line 4 efficiency dropping to 66% during the improvised packaging handling period. This is separate from the raw material cut — it's the efficiency cost of the workaround.

Would it be worth a quick post-mortem on Line 4 to understand if there are process controls we can put in place for future shortage events that would protect line efficiency? Happy to share the production data the Supply Planning Agent has from this week.

Thanks

drivers.name: Near-Expiry Inventory — FEFO Conflict
drivers.description: 2,800 CS of Pedigree Small Dog at DC-02 (Dallas) are within 18 days of expiry. FEFO logic requires first allocation but Kroger MRSL (30 days) prohibits it. Cases are at risk of write-off if not diverted. Supply Planning Agent recommends diversion to Petsmart (15-day MRSL — compliant).
drivers.emailDraft:
Hi James,

DC-02 (Dallas) is holding 2,800 CS of Pedigree Small Dog Chicken with 18 days remaining shelf life. FEFO requires we allocate these first, but Kroger's 30-day MRSL makes them non-compliant for the open Kroger PO.

The Supply Planning Agent recommends diverting these cases to Petsmart, whose 15-day MRSL they comply with. Can you confirm DC-02 can execute the transfer and relabeling within the next 48 hours to avoid a write-off? This resolves the Petsmart PO simultaneously.

Thanks

safetyStockRecommendations.detail: Demand Planning Agent and Retail Intelligence Agent jointly detect promotional volatility on this SKU causing the static 1,800 CS safety stock buffer to breach 3 times in the last 6 weeks. Current static target does not account for regional promotional activity. AI recommends shift to dynamic 2,600 CS average band.
safetyStockRecommendations.rationale: Demand Planning & Supply Planning Joint Recommendation: The current 1,800 CS flat buffer is designed for baseline demand. Promotional events are not consistently captured in Anaplan in advance, creating unhedged demand spikes. A dynamic 2,600 CS band absorbs a 40% demand spike without breaching — consistent with observed promo lift patterns over the last quarter.

safetyStockRecommendations.shortDesc: Overstocked — Reduce Target + Release Working Capital
safetyStockRecommendations.detail: Retail Intelligence Agent notes sustained demand flattening on this SKU over the last 8 weeks. Static safety stock remains at 4,500 CS despite average weekly demand of 1,900 CS. Supply Planning Agent recommends reducing to a dynamic 2,800 CS target to release trapped working capital without impacting 98% CFR guardrail.
safetyStockRecommendations.rationale: Supply Planning & Retail Intelligence Joint Recommendation: Demand has stabilized at an average 1,900 CS per week across all accounts. The current 4,500 CS static target represents 2.4 weeks of cover — far above the 1.2 weeks required to maintain 98% CFR given current supplier lead times. Reducing to a dynamic 2,800 CS average band safely maintains the CFR guardrail while releasing $52,000 in working capital.

safetyStockRecommendations.shortDesc: FEFO Conflict Risk — Shelf Life Buffer Needed
safetyStockRecommendations.detail: Supply Planning Agent identifies a recurring pattern where near-expiry inventory creates MRSL compliance conflicts at Tier 1 accounts. The current static safety stock of 1,200 CS does not account for shelf-life-adjusted availability — meaning usable inventory is consistently lower than the raw count suggests. AI recommends 1,600 CS dynamic target with FEFO-adjusted calculation.
safetyStockRecommendations.rationale: Supply Planning & Retail Intelligence Joint Recommendation: Raw case count at 1,200 CS appears sufficient but FEFO-adjusted usable inventory (excluding near-expiry cases non-compliant with Tier 1 MRSL requirements) is frequently below 800 CS — a 33% effective buffer reduction. A 1,600 CS dynamic target with FEFO-aware calculation provides genuine 1,200 CS of Tier 1-compliant buffer.

safetyStockRecommendations.detail: Minor seasonal adjustment recommended by Supply Planning Agent to compensate for a confirmed 3-day increase in vendor lead time for Q3. Current 1,400 CS static target will result in a sub-98% CFR probability if not adjusted before the lead time change takes effect in June.
safetyStockRecommendations.rationale: Supply Planning Recommendation: Vendor lead time for Whiskas Purrfectly Chicken increases by 3 days in Q3 per confirmed vendor communication. A 250 CS increase in the safety stock target (to 1,650 CS dynamic average) compensates for the additional exposure window. Adjustment should be in place before June 1.
